motivations: prevent a man from becoming a career criminal

motivations: steal the jewel for sudden wealth
avoid prison and save his family's honor
properties.narrative_arc: Confesses to Holmes, hides the jewel in a goose, loses it due to a mix-up, breaks down, and is released; left terrified but presumably reformed
properties.distinctive_qualities: A 'branded thief' who never actually touched the wealth; his extreme fear becomes his salvation

personality_traits: previously involved in minor similar matters
properties.narrative_arc: Arrested for the jewel robbery due to a frame-up; the case against him collapses when Ryder is caught and released
properties.distinctive_qualities: Target of suspicion due to past record; victim of a deliberate frame-up

properties.narrative_arc: Helps Ryder frame Horner and steal the jewel; mentioned as part of the initial crime setup
properties.distinctive_qualities: Ryder's partner in the theft and framing

motivations: provide for her household and sell fowls to the market
properties.narrative_arc: Gives Ryder a goose, unaware of his crime; her practical mistake (mixing up geese) causes Ryder's downfall
properties.distinctive_qualities: Fattens fowls for the market; refers to the chosen goose as 'Jem's bird'

properties.narrative_arc: Mentioned only as Maggie's husband; his house in Brixton Road serves as Ryder's temporary hideout
properties.distinctive_qualities: Absent from the scene; represents the domestic setting where the crime's aftermath unfolds

personality_traits: reliable to Ryder due to leverage
motivations: help Ryder turn the stolen stone into money
properties.narrative_arc: Served time in Pentonville; Ryder seeks him out in Kilburn to fence the jewel; they open the goose but find the stone missing
properties.distinctive_qualities: Lives in Kilburn; knows the ways of thieves; holds secrets over him that ensure his cooperation

properties.narrative_arc: Buys the geese from Maggie's yard, sells them immediately, refuses to reveal where they went, blocking Ryder's recovery of the stone
properties.distinctive_qualities: Operates in Covent Garden; known for giving one-word answers or silence to inquiries

properties.narrative_arc: Witnesses the confession and Holmes's decision to release Ryder; serves as the audience surrogate
properties.distinctive_qualities: Referred to as 'my friend' by Holmes; present during the interrogation

properties.physical_description: Hotel room belonging to a lady

properties.symbolic_meaning: Represents vulnerability and the target of greed

properties.narrative_function: Reference point for Maudsley's criminal record; represents the fate Ryder fears
